## 10. Intentional quirks and non-obvious decisions

### One employee can have many auth identities

Looks like:
`employees` still has deprecated auth columns, so `employee_identities` looks redundant.

Actually:
`employee_identities` is the real auth-link table. The deprecated columns remain only as transition leftovers.

Why:
One person may log in through Google and Microsoft 365 and still be the same employee.

Do not change because:
Collapsing back to one auth identity per employee breaks real user linkage and RLS helpers.

### All inference must go through `OracleAIClient`

Looks like:
It would be simpler for routes or workers to call provider SDKs directly.

Actually:
The provider adapters are the only supported inference boundary. They own prompt shaping, provider-native caching, reasoning translation, and usage normalization.

Why:
The project depends on provider-specific caching and structured-output behavior that generic wrappers and ad hoc calls hide.

Do not change because:
Direct SDK calls bypass context-pack logging, cache observability, route fallback, and consistent validation.

### OpenRouter is enrichment-only

Looks like:
`openrouter.ts` and OpenRouter-related code mean inference still runs through OpenRouter.

Actually:
OpenRouter is only used to enrich the admin-side model catalog with pricing/capability metadata.

Why:
Inference requires native provider features and exact usage fields; catalog enrichment does not.

Do not change because:
Reintroducing OpenRouter into the inference path would erase provider-native cache and usage behavior that the rest of the system expects.

### Explicit Vertex caches are tracked in Postgres

Looks like:
The adapter could rely only on in-memory cache handles.

Actually:
`provider_cached_content` is the cross-process source of truth for explicit Vertex cache lifecycle, and `provider_metadata_json` now also tracks cleanup metadata for temporary GCS-backed cache sources.

Why:
Workers and web requests run in different processes and time windows. Cache accounting and cleanup have to survive process boundaries.

Do not change because:
Process-local cache tracking leaks money and makes cache reuse/cleanup invisible.

### Qwen chat cache state is persisted separately from run usage

Looks like:
`provider_response_sessions` duplicates data already in `model_runs`.

Actually:
`model_runs` is audit history; `provider_response_sessions` stores the latest reusable `previous_response_id` keyed by logical session.

Why:
Responses API session cache needs a stable conversation handle across requests and processes.

Do not change because:
Without it, Qwen session cache resets every turn and the savings disappear.

### Batch API methods on the adapter contract are optional

Looks like:
`OracleProviderAdapter.submitBatch` and `retrieveBatch` are marked `?` (optional). Existing adapters (Anthropic, DeepSeek, Qwen) don't implement them. The interface looks half-finished.

Actually:
The methods are optional on purpose. DeepSeek has no public Batch API. Qwen's batch surface is non-OpenAI-compatible (DECISIONS.md D12 deferred the native DashScope SDK swap). OpenAI, Vertex, and Anthropic implement both methods today; future adapters opt in by implementing both.

Why:
Forcing every adapter to implement batch would either block adding new providers behind a 50%-discount feature, or paper over it with stub `submitBatch` methods that throw — both worse than the optional pattern. The runtime helper `supportsBatch(adapter)` is the feature-detection contract. See DECISIONS.md D14.

Do not change because:
Making `submitBatch` / `retrieveBatch` required on the interface would force the DeepSeek and Qwen adapters to throw at construction, which would break the per-provider `tryAdd()` boot in `buildStandardAdapters()` and silently disable both providers.

Anthropic batch specifics: each request is a `messages.batches.create` entry with `custom_id` + `params` (same shape as a sync `messages.create`); when `jsonSchema` is provided we attach the forced single-tool input_schema per-request (mirrors `generateObject`). Status maps `processing_status: ended` → `'completed'` and we stream `messages.batches.results(id)` to produce `BatchResultItem`s. Per-item `result.type` → success (`succeeded` — tool_use input or text), or failure (`errored` / `canceled` / `expired`). `providerMetadata` is `{}` — the batch ID alone is sufficient for retrieve.

### Two-phase batch worker — submit and drain are independent tasks

Looks like:
`claim-extraction.ts`, `claim-extraction-batch-submit.ts`, and `claim-extraction-batch-drain.ts` look redundant. The flag `extraction_dispatch_mode` toggles them, but all three are scheduled.

Actually:
Each task reads `settings.extraction_dispatch_mode` at the top and bails when it doesn't match its dispatch mode. So at any given time only ONE of (sync) or (submit + drain) is active. The drain task is always scheduled because it always needs to poll outstanding `provider_batch_jobs` rows even after the flag flips back to `'sync'` (any in-flight batches should still be drained, not orphaned).

Why:
The flag is read every cron tick — flipping it doesn't require a redeploy. The drain task running unconditionally ensures no orphaned batches when admin flips back to sync. Per-task short-circuit at the top is the cleanest gate.

Do not change because:
Removing the always-on drain task would orphan any in-flight Vertex/OpenAI batches if the flag flipped back to sync mid-stream. Removing the gate from the sync task would double-process in batch mode.

### Model catalog quality filter runs at BOTH write and read time

Looks like:
The same filter logic appears in `packages/ai/src/model-capabilities/index.ts` (`refreshModelCatalog`) AND `apps/web/app/api/admin/model-catalog/route.ts` (`passesQualityFilter`). Looks duplicated.

Actually:
Intentional defense in depth. The write-time filter prevents new junk from landing in the DB. The read-time filter handles the legacy case: rows written BEFORE the write-time filter shipped (or when OpenRouter enrichment was unavailable) sit in the DB with no pricing and no capability flags. Deleting them would break pool selections that still reference deprecated model IDs. Filtering at read time hides them from admins without losing the FK target.

Why:
The write-time-only filter let junk creep back into the admin UI whenever an old OpenRouter outage produced unenriched rows. The read-time filter is the catch-all.

Do not change because:
Removing the read-time filter resurrects the original "junky models reappear" bug. Removing the write-time filter floods the DB with no-data rows that build up over time. Keep both.

### Design file operations are not product/design workflow

Looks like:
Design-file naming, server organization, invalid filename characters, Photoshop/Illustrator file bloat, linked assets, packaging, and archive cleanup could live under `creative_design`, `product_development`, or `it_systems` because the design team and design tools are involved.

Actually:
They live in the dedicated top-level domain `design_file_operations`. That domain is for technical creative-file hygiene: keeping design files valid, lightweight, findable, compatible, packaged, versioned, and safe to share. Product/design workflow remains separate: concept intake, design assignment, proofs, approvals, revisions, production handoff, and product lifecycle state belong in `product_development`, `creative_design`, `licensing_approvals`, or `production_lifecycle`.

Why:
The same employees participate in both knowledge bases, but the user intent is different. "How should I name/save/store this file?" should not retrieve product approval/status claims. "Where is this product in design approval?" should not retrieve filename/server-folder rules.

Do not change because:
Collapsing these domains causes retrieval bleed between computer/file-management practices and the business workflow of designs/products moving through the company. Keep `design_file_operations` as its own retrieval target and preserve its negative boundary against product workflow domains.

### Ineligible models are SELECTABLE (red checkbox), not disabled

Looks like:
Models that don't meet a stage's required capabilities still have an active checkbox in the model-pool grid, just colored red.

Actually:
Admin override is intentional. Sometimes a model is fine for a stage even when one of the canonical capability flags is missing (e.g. OpenRouter hasn't enriched the model yet, but the admin knows it supports tools). Disabling the checkbox would force admins to wait for enrichment or edit settings via SQL.

Why:
The stage-requirements predicates are a heuristic, not ground truth. Red styling + the missing-caps hover tooltip make the override state obvious; no silent risk of accidental selection.

Do not change because:
Disabling the checkbox blocks valid admin overrides and forces SQL editing.

### Stage `thinking` requirement is on Synthesis, not Extraction

Looks like:
The Extraction model card has no reasoning-effort row visible, while Synthesis does — even though extraction is the more "thinking-heavy" task by feel.

Actually:
`thinking` was moved from `STAGE_REQUIREMENTS.extraction` → `STAGE_REQUIREMENTS.synthesis` on 2026-05-28 (`apps/web/lib/stage-requirements.ts`). Extraction benefits more from speed + verbatim quote fidelity; synthesis benefits from extended reasoning when consolidating a large approved-claim corpus into a single Brain section.

Why:
Earlier requirement set forced extraction into reasoning models that produced slower, more elaborate JSON for no provable accuracy win. Moving `thinking` to synthesis matches actual cost/quality observations.

Do not change because:
Putting `thinking` back on extraction excludes Gemini Flash and the cheap GPT-4o-mini path from the extraction pool — both proven good for extraction in our wet-tests.

### OpenAI model catalog uses a blocklist, not an allowlist

Looks like:
A new GPT or o-series model is missing from the admin catalog even though it appears in the OpenAI API response.

Actually:
The source in `packages/ai/src/model-capabilities/sources/openai.ts` uses a blocklist of non-chat categories. A model whose name hits a blocked prefix or substring (`-tts`, `-transcribe`, `-search-api`, etc.) is excluded. Otherwise it passes through automatically.

Why:
An allowlist required a code change for every new OpenAI model generation. GPT-5.x was invisible until `gpt-5` was manually added to the prefix list. The blocklist lets new chat models appear without a code change; only genuinely non-chat categories are excluded. See `DECISIONS.md` D13.

Do not change because:
Reverting to an allowlist means future GPT-6/o-series models will be silently missing from the catalog until someone notices and edits the list. Post-enrichment quality filters (no-data models, ≥$15.01/1M input) provide a second layer of junk removal in `index.ts`.

### Hand-written SQL migrations are authoritative for constraints, views, and data fixes

Looks like:
Drizzle-generated SQL should be enough, so editing old generated files might be acceptable.

Actually:
Generated files cover schema DDL only. Constraints, RLS, views, and data migrations live in `packages/db/migrations/sql/*.sql`.

Why:
The migration runner applies generated DDL plus hand-written SQL in deterministic order.

Do not change because:
Editing old generated files breaks replay expectations and production drift recovery.

### Claim retrieval has exactly one path, and the two SQL branches must stay in lockstep

Looks like:
`searchWithRetrievalPlan()` in `packages/ai/src/retrieval.ts` contains two near-duplicate SQL queries — a hybrid pgvector+tsvector path and a `_searchFallbackTsvector()` path — and a separate `verify:retrieval-filter-parity` script that seems redundant with typecheck.

Actually:
The fallback runs only when `OPENAI_API_KEY` is unset (dev, no embeddings). Both branches build their WHERE clauses from the same private helper `buildPlanMetadataFilters()`, and the parity guard statically asserts every filter the helper returns is interpolated into BOTH branches. `searchApprovedClaims()` (a weaker, plan-less retrieval) was deleted on 2026-05-28 — there is now exactly one endorsed retrieval entry point.

Why:
The recurring regression here was adding a narrowing filter to the hybrid path and forgetting the fallback, making dev-mode retrieval silently weaker (or vice versa). Typecheck can't catch it — the fragments are SQL strings. The guard runs in CI (`pr-check.yml`) and via `pnpm --filter @oracle/ai verify:retrieval-filter-parity`.

Do not change because:
Adding a second retrieval path (or a filter to only one branch) reintroduces the exact silent-divergence class the guard exists to prevent. New narrowing fields go in `buildPlanMetadataFilters()` and get interpolated into both branches — nowhere else.

### Microsoft Graph Teams transcripts arrive after the call

Looks like:
The Oracle could "sit in" a Teams call and react live, or read the live transcript panel a bot can see on screen.

Actually:
Microsoft Graph exposes **no** live caption/transcript API, and Teams does not pipe spoken words into the meeting text chat. The Graph transcript path is therefore after-the-fact, and only if transcription was turned on. Ad-hoc "Meet Now" calls are reachable **only** through a `communications/adhocCalls/getAllTranscripts` change-notification subscription (beta endpoint; v1.0 rejects it), which is "listen going forward" — a transcript notifies only if the subscription existed before transcription started.

Live spoken participation now exists through the optional Recall.ai path: Recall provides the meeting bot and STT stream; The Oracle receives finalized `transcript.data` utterances via `/api/teams/live/recall`, writes them as `messages`, and posts only gated questions back to Teams chat through Recall.

Why:
Native live spoken awareness would require Microsoft media-bot infrastructure. Recall avoids changing Oracle's infrastructure by externalizing the Teams audio/STT transport while keeping Oracle's durable state in Postgres.

Do not change because:
Do not confuse the two paths. Graph is post-call evidence/backfill; Recall is live optional participation. Both must still write utterances to `messages` and let candidate-before-claim handle durable knowledge. See `docs/architecture.md` § "Teams transcript ingestion" and § "Teams live participation (Recall.ai)".

### The Graph subscription/transcript helper is duplicated on purpose

Looks like:
`apps/web/lib/microsoft-graph.ts` and `apps/workers/src/lib/graph-transcripts.ts` both implement the app-only token + subscription/transcript calls. Looks like copy-paste that should be a shared package.

Actually:
`apps/web` (the webhook) and `apps/workers` (the subscription manager + ingestion) are separate runtime processes, and cross-app imports aren't allowed. Both need the same small Graph surface. The web copy (`microsoft-graph.ts`) is the reference; the worker copy is intentionally self-contained.

Do not change because:
Forcing a shared package for ~150 lines pulls Graph code into a third location and couples the web and worker builds. If they drift, reconcile toward the web copy rather than introducing a shared dependency.

### ESLint config imports eslint-config-next natively, not via FlatCompat

Looks like:
`apps/web/eslint.config.mjs` imports `eslint-config-next/core-web-vitals` and spreads it directly, instead of the `FlatCompat`-based pattern `create-next-app` generates.

Actually:
With `eslint-config-next` v16 + ESLint 9, the `FlatCompat` path throws `TypeError: Converting circular structure to JSON` (the bundled react config has a circular `configs` object). v16 ships a native flat-config array, so importing it directly is the working path. The old `.eslintrc.json` + `next lint` were removed (Next 16 dropped the `next lint` subcommand).

Do not change because:
Reverting to `FlatCompat` reintroduces the circular-structure crash; reverting to `next lint` breaks entirely (`next lint` no longer exists in Next 16).

### Quote validation is fuzzy for transcripts, strict for documents

Looks like:
`validateQuote` has an `allowFuzzy` path that accepts a quote when its tokens merely *overlap* the source — contradicting the "deterministic verbatim provenance / no fuzzy match" principle in `docs/oracle/03` and the comment at the top of `quote-validator.ts`.

Actually:
Spoken Teams transcripts are disfluent and the extraction model paraphrases them, so the polished claim quote never appears verbatim in any utterance — strict matching rejected ~every transcript-derived claim. The fuzzy path (opt-in, enabled only on the message/transcript path in `claim-extraction.ts`; documents stay strict) is a **deterministic** token-overlap check (no LLM grader) and anchors the stored evidence to the **real** utterance text, not the model's paraphrase. See `DECISIONS.md` D-transcript-fuzzy-quote.

Why:
Without it the entire Teams-transcript feature produces zero promotable claims. Provenance is preserved as "this real utterance supports this claim" rather than "the model copied these exact words."

Do not change because:
Reverting to strict-only re-breaks transcript extraction. If tightening is wanted, raise `fuzzyMinOverlap` or restrict `allowFuzzy` to transcript-sourced messages — don't remove it.

### `raw_transcripts` is hand-written SQL, not in schema.ts

Looks like:
`raw_transcripts` (the original VTT per call) is missing from `packages/db/src/schema.ts`, and the ingestion worker reads/writes it with raw `sql` instead of Drizzle.

Actually:
It's defined only in the hand-written `packages/db/migrations/sql/62_raw_transcripts.sql` (idempotent `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS`), like the observability views. Keeping it out of `schema.ts` avoids a drizzle-kit drift entry for an ancillary raw-storage table the typed query layer never needs.

Why:
The VTT is stored so the whole pipeline stays re-runnable from true source after Microsoft expires the transcript (`messages` are a lossy transform). See `DECISIONS.md` D-raw-transcripts.

Do not change because:
Adding it to `schema.ts` would make drizzle-kit want to generate a migration for an already-applied hand-written table (drift). Leave it as hand-written SQL.

## 12. Deployment

Current deployment path:

- GitHub repo: `u2giants/theoracle`
- CI workflow: `.github/workflows/pr-check.yml`
- Web deploy target: Vercel project `prj_rP6Jlima7iK1paffEPhLqxlswGsC`
- Worker deploy target: Trigger.dev project `proj_wgpzsvhmsopqhvwqaycn`
- Database/auth/storage target: Supabase project configured through env

How deployment works today:

1. Merge or push to `main`.
2. GitHub Actions runs `pr-check.yml`: installs dependencies, builds `@oracle/web`, runs the static DB-free verify guards (`verify:retrieval-filter-parity`, `verify:vertex-file-cache`), then runs `pnpm db:check-drift` against production (requires the `PROD_DIRECT_URL` repo secret; skips gracefully if absent). Drift in the Drizzle migration journal fails the build.
3. Vercel auto-deploys the web app from GitHub using `vercel.json`.
4. Trigger.dev workers are deployed manually with `pnpm --filter @oracle/workers run deploy` (the `run` keyword is required — `pnpm` reserves the bare `deploy` form for its own subcommand).
5. Database migrations are applied manually with `pnpm db:migrate` before shipping code that depends on them. Hand-written `migrations/sql/*.sql` files MAY be applied via Supabase MCP `apply_migration`; generated `0NNN_*.sql` files MUST go through `pnpm db:migrate` (otherwise the journal drifts — see incident 2026-05-28).

Rollback:

- Web: promote a previous Vercel deployment.
- Workers: redeploy from a previous commit or roll back in Trigger.dev.
- DB: ship a compensating SQL migration; there is no automatic rollback layer.

Runtime env vars live in:

- Vercel project environment settings
- Trigger.dev project secrets/env
- local `.env.local`

SSH:

- SSH is not part of the normal deployment path.
- No VPS/container SSH workflow exists in this repo.

### Release & CI/CD policy (the rules that actually apply here)

This repo is a **managed-platform** deployment — Vercel (web) + Trigger.dev (workers) + Supabase (DB/auth/storage). There are **no containers, no Dockerfiles, no container registry, no Coolify, no production VPS, and no SSH deploy**. Generic container/registry/Coolify/SSH CI-CD rules are therefore **Not Applicable** unless this repo ever adopts a self-hosted containerized model. The rules that DO apply:

- **Single-branch model: work on `main` only.** Do **not** create feature, staging, or release branches, and do not open PRs as a routine workflow — this repo has no promotion model. Commit straight to `main`. (Push to `main` only when Albert says push — see CLAUDE.md.)
- **One release path, repo-driven:** push to `main` → Vercel builds/deploys the web app from `vercel.json`; workers ship via `pnpm --filter @oracle/workers run deploy`; DB via `pnpm db:migrate`. No alternate routine deploy method.
- **CI verifies, never deploys.** `pr-check.yml` only builds + runs the static verify guards + checks migration drift. It must not deploy, SSH, mutate production, or publish artifacts.
- **The deploy gate is native to Vercel's build.** `vercel.json` `buildCommand` runs the static verify guards (`verify:retrieval-filter-parity`, `verify:vertex-file-cache`) **before** `@oracle/web build`. If a guard or the build fails, Vercel's production build fails and the previous deployment stays live — so a guard-breaking or build-breaking commit cannot reach production. This is the "hard blocker": enforcement lives in the platform's own build, not in a separate CI deploy step.
- **Repo is authoritative; the platform owns runtime.** Runtime env vars / secrets / domains live in Vercel / Trigger.dev / Supabase — never baked into CI shell commands, images, or committed `.env*` (except `.env.example`).
- **Schema changes only through the approved migration path** (`pnpm db:migrate` for generated `0NNN_*.sql`; Supabase MCP `apply_migration` for hand-written `sql/*.sql`). Never ad-hoc production schema edits as the normal path.
- **Traceability:** every production change is auditable from repo commit history + Vercel / Trigger.dev / Supabase deployment history.
- **Drift check is advisory.** The Drizzle migration-drift check needs prod DB creds and runs only in `pr-check.yml`, not in the Vercel build (the build has no prod DB access, by design). Migrations are applied manually via `pnpm db:migrate` anyway, so drift is a bookkeeping signal, not a runtime-breakage gate. Everything else (build + verify guards) is hard-gated in the Vercel build per the bullet above.

## 13. Critical incidents

18	//     3. Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF, k=60) to combine both signals.
19	//   The fallback tsvector path (_searchFallbackTsvector) MUST stay behaviorally
20	//   identical for filtering — both paths build their WHERE clauses from the

23	import { and, desc, eq, inArray, or, sql } from 'drizzle-orm';
24	import {

27	  channelParticipants,

35	import type { OracleDb } from '@oracle/db/client';

37	import { embedText } from './embeddings';
38	import { type RetrievalPlan } from './retrieval-plan';
39	
40	// Use the full-schema-aware DB type so callers passing in the result of
41	// getDirectDb() / getPooledDb() type-check without casts. The retrieval helpers
42	// don't actually need the schema at the type level — they hand-write SQL via
43	// the bare drizzle-orm builders — but accepting a wider type kept the Vercel
44	// production build failing on a generic mismatch.
45	type Db = OracleDb;
46	
47	export const DEFAULT_RECENT_MESSAGES = 30;
48	export const DEFAULT_GAPS_LIMIT = 5;
49	
50	/**
51	 * Build the shared metadata WHERE fragments used by both the hybrid and
52	 * tsvector-fallback retrieval paths. Both paths use `c` as the claims alias,
53	 * `ctd` as the claim_top_domains join alias, and `cm` as the claim_metadata
54	 * join alias. Keep those alias names stable in the consuming queries.
55	 *
56	 * Important: requiredEntities matches on both (entity_type, canonical_value)
57	 * — matching canonical_value alone lets a name registered under a different
58	 * type incorrectly match.
59	 */
60	function buildPlanMetadataFilters(plan: RetrievalPlan) {
61	  const topDomainFilter =
62	    plan.topDomainHints.length > 0
63	      ? sql`AND ctd.top_domain_id = ANY(${plan.topDomainHints}::text[])`
64	      : sql``;
65	
66	  // Exclude claims that carry ANY excluded top-domain. claim_top_domains is
67	  // multi-valued, so a direct predicate on the LEFT-JOINed ctd row would let
68	  // a claim survive through a different, non-excluded domain row. NOT EXISTS
69	  // against the full set is the only correct form. Composes with
70	  // topDomainFilter: a claim must (match a hint, if hints given) AND (carry no
71	  // excluded domain).
72	  const excludedTopDomainFilter =
73	    plan.excludedTopDomains && plan.excludedTopDomains.length > 0
74	      ? sql`AND NOT EXISTS (
75	          SELECT 1 FROM claim_top_domains _xtd
76	          WHERE _xtd.claim_id = c.id
77	            AND _xtd.top_domain_id = ANY(${plan.excludedTopDomains}::text[])
78	        )`
79	      : sql``;
80	
81	  const docClassFilter =
82	    plan.excludedDocumentClasses && plan.excludedDocumentClasses.length > 0
83	      ? sql`AND (cm.document_class IS NULL OR NOT (cm.document_class = ANY(${plan.excludedDocumentClasses}::text[])))`
84	      : sql``;
85	
86	  const timeFilter =
87	    plan.timeFilter === 'current'
88	      ? sql`AND (cm.id IS NULL OR cm.effective_until IS NULL)`
89	      : plan.timeFilter && plan.timeFilter.startsWith('since:')
90	        ? (() => {
91	            const since = plan.timeFilter.slice(6); // 'YYYY-MM-DD'
92	            return sql`AND (cm.effective_from IS NULL OR cm.effective_from >= ${since}::timestamptz)`;
93	          })()
94	        : sql``;
95	
96	  // Narrowing filter (not a ranking hint): keep only claims whose
97	  // claim_metadata.process_stage is one of the requested stages. claim_metadata
98	  // is 1:1 with claims, so this predicates directly on the LEFT-JOINed cm row.
99	  // A claim with no metadata row (cm.process_stage IS NULL) does not satisfy a
100	  // specific-stage requirement and is correctly dropped — `NULL = ANY(...)`
101	  // evaluates to NULL, which is not TRUE.
102	  const processStageFilter =
103	    plan.processStageHints && plan.processStageHints.length > 0
104	      ? sql`AND cm.process_stage = ANY(${plan.processStageHints}::text[])`
105	      : sql``;
106	
107	  const excludedEntityTypeFilter =
108	    plan.excludedEntityTypes && plan.excludedEntityTypes.length > 0
109	      ? sql`AND NOT EXISTS (
110	          SELECT 1 FROM claim_entities _ce
111	          JOIN entities _e ON _e.id = _ce.entity_id
112	          WHERE _ce.claim_id = c.id
113	            AND _e.entity_type = ANY(${plan.excludedEntityTypes}::text[])
114	        )`
115	      : sql``;
116	
117	  // Tuple match on (entity_type, canonical_value) so a canonical_value
118	  // registered under a different entity_type cannot incidentally match.
119	  // unnest(arr1, arr2) zips the parallel arrays into a derived table.
120	  const requiredEntityFilter =
121	    plan.requiredEntities.length > 0
122	      ? sql`AND EXISTS (
123	          SELECT 1
124	          FROM claim_entities _ce2
125	          JOIN entities _e2 ON _e2.id = _ce2.entity_id
126	          JOIN unnest(
127	            ${plan.requiredEntities.map((e) => e.entityType)}::text[],
128	            ${plan.requiredEntities.map((e) => e.canonicalValue)}::text[]
129	          ) AS _req(t, v)
130	            ON _e2.entity_type = _req.t
131	           AND _e2.canonical_value = _req.v
132	          WHERE _ce2.claim_id = c.id
133	        )`
134	      : sql``;
135	
136	  return {
137	    topDomainFilter,
138	    excludedTopDomainFilter,
139	    docClassFilter,
140	    timeFilter,
141	    processStageFilter,
142	    excludedEntityTypeFilter,
143	    requiredEntityFilter,
144	  };
145	}

173	      role: messages.role,
174	      content: messages.content,
175	      createdAt: messages.createdAt,
176	      authorName: employees.name,
177	    })
178	    .from(messages)
179	    .leftJoin(employees, eq(messages.employeeId, employees.id))
180	    .where(eq(messages.channelId, channelId))
181	    .orderBy(desc(messages.createdAt))
182	    .limit(limit);
183	  return rows.reverse();
184	}
185	
186	/** Open gaps relevant to this employee, in priority order. */
187	export async function getRelevantOpenGaps(
188	  db: Db,
189	  employee: Employee,
190	  limit = DEFAULT_GAPS_LIMIT,
191	): Promise<Gap[]> {
192	  const depts = employee.departments.length > 0
193	    ? employee.departments
194	    : employee.department ? [employee.department] : [];
195	
196	  const rows = await db
197	    .select()
198	    .from(gaps)
199	    .where(
200	      and(
201	        inArray(gaps.status, ['open', 'queued', 'asked']),
202	        or(
203	          eq(gaps.targetEmployeeId, employee.id),
204	          depts.length > 0
205	            ? inArray(gaps.targetDepartment, depts)
206	            : undefined,
207	        ),
208	      ),
209	    )
210	    .orderBy(
211	      sql`CASE ${gaps.priority}
212	            WHEN 'urgent' THEN 0
213	            WHEN 'high'   THEN 1
214	            WHEN 'medium' THEN 2
215	            ELSE 3 END`,
216	      desc(gaps.createdAt),
217	    )
218	    .limit(limit);
219	  return rows;
220	}
221	
222	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
223	// Hybrid RRF retrieval — P1 #3
224	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
225	
226	/**
227	 * Hybrid pgvector + tsvector retrieval with Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF).
228	 *
229	 * Implements the full RetrievalPlan spec from docs/oracle/07-knowledge-segmentation.md.
230	 * Filters the candidate set by top-level domain, entity type exclusions,
231	 * document class exclusions, and time validity BEFORE vector search.
232	 *
233	 * When no real embeddings are available (OPENAI_API_KEY not set), falls back
234	 * to pure tsvector ranking + metadata filter so retrieval still works in dev.
235	 *
236	 * @example
237	 * const plan = buildRetrievalPlanFromQuery("When does artwork go to China?");
238	 * const results = await searchWithRetrievalPlan(db, plan);
239	 */
240	export async function searchWithRetrievalPlan(
241	  db: Db,
242	  plan: RetrievalPlan,
243	): Promise<RelevantClaim[]> {
244	  // Enforcement: every global-fallback plan emits a structured warning so
245	  // operators can audit oracle_context_packs.selected_domains and improve
246	  // the DOMAIN_KEYWORDS heuristics in retrieval-plan.ts.
247	  if (plan.searchScope === 'global_fallback') {
248	    console.warn('[oracle:retrieval] global_fallback — searching entire claim corpus', {
249	      query: plan.vectorQuery.slice(0, 120),
250	      hint: 'Add matching keywords to DOMAIN_KEYWORDS in packages/ai/src/retrieval-plan.ts',
251	      impact: 'vendor_manual / licensor noise may contaminate results',
252	    });
253	  }
254	
255	  const limit = plan.topK;
256	  const { vector, fallback } = plan.precomputedVector
257	    ? { vector: plan.precomputedVector, fallback: false }
258	    : await embedText(plan.vectorQuery);
259	
260	  if (fallback) {
261	    return _searchFallbackTsvector(db, plan, limit);
262	  }
263	
264	  const vec = `[${vector.join(',')}]`;
265	  const textQuery = plan.vectorQuery;
266	
267	  const {
268	    topDomainFilter,
269	    excludedTopDomainFilter,
270	    docClassFilter,
271	    timeFilter,
272	    processStageFilter,
273	    excludedEntityTypeFilter,
274	    requiredEntityFilter,
275	  } = buildPlanMetadataFilters(plan);
276	
277	  // Small department-match bonus — joins claim_metadata on department to give
278	  // a soft nudge to claims tagged with the requesting employee's department(s).
279	  // Value (0.002) is intentionally small: roughly equivalent to ~15-rank RRF
280	  // lift at mid-list, so it nudges without overriding semantic relevance.
281	  const deptHints = plan.departmentHints ?? [];
282	  const deptBonusCte =
283	    deptHints.length > 0
284	      ? sql`
285	    dept_bonus AS (
286	      SELECT DISTINCT cm_d.claim_id
287	      FROM claim_metadata cm_d
288	      WHERE cm_d.department = ANY(${deptHints}::text[])
289	    ),`
290	      : sql``;
291	  const deptBonusTerm =
292	    deptHints.length > 0
293	      ? sql`+ CASE WHEN db.claim_id IS NOT NULL THEN 0.002 ELSE 0.0 END`
294	      : sql``;
295	  const deptBonusJoin =
296	    deptHints.length > 0
297	      ? sql`LEFT JOIN dept_bonus db ON db.claim_id = vr.id`
298	      : sql``;
299	
300	  const rows = await db.execute<{
301	    id: string;
302	    summary: string;
303	    claim_type: string;
304	    impact_score: number;
305	    confidence_score: number;
306	    distance: number;
307	    rrf_score: number;
308	  }>(sql`
309	    WITH
310	    pre_filtered AS (
311	      SELECT DISTINCT
312	        c.id, c.summary, c.claim_type, c.impact_score, c.confidence_score, c.embedding
313	      FROM claims c
314	      LEFT JOIN claim_top_domains ctd ON ctd.claim_id = c.id
315	      LEFT JOIN claim_metadata    cm  ON cm.claim_id  = c.id
316	      WHERE c.status = 'approved'
317	        AND c.embedding IS NOT NULL
318	        ${topDomainFilter}
319	        ${excludedTopDomainFilter}
320	        ${docClassFilter}
321	        ${timeFilter}
322	        ${processStageFilter}
323	        ${excludedEntityTypeFilter}
324	        ${requiredEntityFilter}
325	    ),
326	    vec_ranked AS (
327	      SELECT
328	        id, summary, claim_type, impact_score, confidence_score,
329	        (embedding <=> ${vec}::vector(${EMBEDDING_DIM})) AS vec_dist,
330	        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
331	          ORDER BY embedding <=> ${vec}::vector(${EMBEDDING_DIM})
332	        ) AS vrank
333	      FROM pre_filtered
334	    ),
335	    txt_scored AS (
336	      SELECT
337	        id,
338	        ts_rank(
339	          to_tsvector('english', summary),
340	          plainto_tsquery('english', ${textQuery})
341	        ) AS ts_score
342	      FROM pre_filtered
343	      WHERE to_tsvector('english', summary) @@ plainto_tsquery('english', ${textQuery})
344	    ),
345	    txt_ranked AS (
346	      SELECT id, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY ts_score DESC) AS trank
347	      FROM txt_scored
348	    ),
349	    ${deptBonusCte}
350	    rrf AS (
351	      SELECT
352	        vr.id, vr.summary, vr.claim_type, vr.impact_score, vr.confidence_score,
353	        vr.vec_dist,
354	        1.0 / (60.0 + vr.vrank::float) +
355	        COALESCE(1.0 / (60.0 + tr.trank::float), 0.0)
356	        ${deptBonusTerm} AS rrf_score
357	      FROM vec_ranked vr
358	      LEFT JOIN txt_ranked tr ON tr.id = vr.id
359	      ${deptBonusJoin}
360	    )
361	    SELECT id, summary, claim_type, impact_score, confidence_score,
362	           vec_dist AS distance, rrf_score
363	    FROM rrf
364	    ORDER BY rrf_score DESC
365	    LIMIT ${limit}
366	  `);
367	
368	  return rows.map((r) => ({
369	    id: r.id,
370	    summary: r.summary,
371	    claimType: r.claim_type,
372	    impactScore: r.impact_score,
373	    confidenceScore: r.confidence_score,
374	    distance: r.distance,
375	  }));
376	}
377	
378	/**
379	 * Tsvector-only fallback used when OPENAI_API_KEY is absent (dev mode).
380	 * Applies the same metadata pre-filters from the plan.
381	 */
382	async function _searchFallbackTsvector(
383	  db: Db,
384	  plan: RetrievalPlan,
385	  limit: number,
386	): Promise<RelevantClaim[]> {
387	  if (plan.searchScope === 'global_fallback') {
388	    console.warn('[oracle:retrieval] global_fallback (tsvector path) — searching entire claim corpus', {
389	      query: plan.vectorQuery.slice(0, 120),
390	      hint: 'Add matching keywords to DOMAIN_KEYWORDS in packages/ai/src/retrieval-plan.ts',
391	    });
392	  }
393	
394	  const textQuery = plan.vectorQuery;
395	
396	  // Shared filter helper — keeps fallback behaviorally identical to the main
397	  // hybrid path for every narrowing field (top-domain hint + exclusion,
398	  // doc-class, time, process-stage, entity-type exclusion, required-entity),
399	  // so the dev fallback isn't silently weaker. Any filter added here MUST be
400	  // consumed in the WHERE clause below too — the parity smoke test
401	  // (verify:retrieval-filter-parity) fails if a key is destructured but unused.
402	  const {
403	    topDomainFilter,
404	    excludedTopDomainFilter,
405	    docClassFilter,
406	    timeFilter,
407	    processStageFilter,
408	    excludedEntityTypeFilter,
409	    requiredEntityFilter,
410	  } = buildPlanMetadataFilters(plan);
411	
412	  const rows = await db.execute<{
413	    id: string;
414	    summary: string;
415	    claim_type: string;
416	    impact_score: number;
417	    confidence_score: number;
418	    ts_score: number;
419	  }>(sql`
420	    SELECT DISTINCT
421	      c.id, c.summary, c.claim_type, c.impact_score, c.confidence_score,
422	      ts_rank(
423	        to_tsvector('english', c.summary),
424	        plainto_tsquery('english', ${textQuery})
425	      ) AS ts_score
426	    FROM claims c
427	    LEFT JOIN claim_top_domains ctd ON ctd.claim_id = c.id
428	    LEFT JOIN claim_metadata    cm  ON cm.claim_id  = c.id
429	    WHERE c.status = 'approved'
430	      ${topDomainFilter}
431	      ${excludedTopDomainFilter}
432	      ${docClassFilter}
433	      ${timeFilter}
434	      ${processStageFilter}
435	      ${excludedEntityTypeFilter}
436	      ${requiredEntityFilter}
437	    ORDER BY ts_score DESC, c.impact_score DESC
438	    LIMIT ${limit}
439	  `);
440	
441	  return rows.map((r) => ({
442	    id: r.id,
443	    summary: r.summary,
444	    claimType: r.claim_type,
445	    impactScore: r.impact_score,
446	    confidenceScore: r.confidence_score,
447	    distance: 1 - r.ts_score, // approximate: lower ts_score → higher distance
448	  }));
449	}
450	
451	/** Fetch the current Markdown for a small set of brain sections. */
452	export async function getBrainSectionSnippets(
453	  db: Db,
454	  sectionIds: string[],
455	): Promise<Array<{ sectionId: string; title: string; markdown: string }>> {
456	  if (sectionIds.length === 0) return [];
457	  const rows = await db
458	    .select({
459	      sectionId: brainSections.id,
460	      title: brainSections.title,
461	      markdown: brainSectionVersions.markdown,
462	    })
463	    .from(brainSections)
464	    .leftJoin(
465	      brainSectionVersions,
466	      eq(brainSectionVersions.id, brainSections.currentVersionId),
467	    )
468	    .where(inArray(brainSections.id, sectionIds));
469	  return rows.map((r) => ({
470	    sectionId: r.sectionId,
471	    title: r.title,
472	    markdown: r.markdown ?? '',
473	  }));
474	}
475	
476	/** Channel-aware gap fetch — used by the check_open_gaps tool. */
477	export async function getOpenGapsForChannel(
478	  db: Db,
479	  channelId: string,
480	  limit = DEFAULT_GAPS_LIMIT,
481	): Promise<Gap[]> {
482	  // Channel members → their departments.
483	  const members = await db
484	    .select({
485	      employeeId: channelParticipants.employeeId,
486	      department: employees.department,
487	      departments: employees.departments,
488	    })
489	    .from(channelParticipants)
490	    .innerJoin(employees, eq(employees.id, channelParticipants.employeeId))
491	    .where(eq(channelParticipants.channelId, channelId));
492	
493	  if (members.length === 0) return [];
494	
495	  const employeeIds = members.map((m) => m.employeeId);
496	  // Flatten all departments from all channel members (both old and new fields).
497	  const departments = Array.from(new Set(
498	    members.flatMap((m) =>
499	      m.departments.length > 0
500	        ? m.departments
501	        : m.department ? [m.department] : [],
502	    ),
503	  ));
504	
505	  return db
506	    .select()
507	    .from(gaps)
508	    .where(
509	      and(
510	        inArray(gaps.status, ['open', 'queued', 'asked']),
511	        or(
512	          inArray(gaps.targetEmployeeId, employeeIds),
513	          departments.length > 0
514	            ? inArray(gaps.targetDepartment, departments)
515	            : undefined,
516	        ),
517	      ),
518	    )
519	    .orderBy(
520	      sql`CASE ${gaps.priority}
521	            WHEN 'urgent' THEN 0
522	            WHEN 'high'   THEN 1
523	            WHEN 'medium' THEN 2
524	            ELSE 3 END`,
525	      desc(gaps.createdAt),
526	    )
527	    .limit(limit);
528	}

1	// RetrievalPlan — spec docs/oracle/07-knowledge-segmentation.md §"What this does to retrieval code".
2	//
3	// Every query to the Oracle knowledge graph MUST go through a RetrievalPlan.
4	// Global vector search without metadata pre-filtering is forbidden (spec rule from
5	// 00-buildout-index.md "Retrieval rule" and 07-knowledge-segmentation.md).
6	//
7	// Enforcement mechanism: the `searchScope` field on every RetrievalPlan records
8	// HOW the scope was determined.  searchWithRetrievalPlan() emits a structured
9	// console.warn for every 'global_fallback' plan so operators can audit
10	//   SELECT ... WHERE selected_domains @> ARRAY['_global_fallback']
11	// in the oracle_context_packs table and improve heuristics accordingly.
12	//
13	// This module provides:
14	//   - RetrievalPlanSearchScope — 'domain_filtered' | 'global_fallback' | 'global_explicit'
15	//   - RetrievalPlan type (matches the spec shape exactly + searchScope).
16	//   - buildRetrievalPlanFromQuery() — lightweight synchronous heuristic builder.
17	//   - buildDomainScopedPlan()       — explicit domain list, no heuristic exclusions.
18	//   - buildGlobalRetrievalPlan()    — intentional all-corpus search.
19	//
20	// A model-backed variant (buildRetrievalPlanWithModel) can be added later when
21	// per-query latency budgets allow an extra cheap structured-output call.
22	
23	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
24	// Types
25	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
26	
27	/**
28	 * How the retrieval scope was determined.
29	 *
30	 *   domain_filtered  — ≥1 domain matched from keyword heuristics or explicit
31	 *                      caller input. Search is scoped; vendor-manual noise is
32	 *                      suppressed from unrelated results.
33	 *   global_fallback  — 0 domains matched. Falling back to full-corpus search.
34	 *                      searchWithRetrievalPlan() logs a structured warning so
35	 *                      the gap can be closed by adding keywords to DOMAIN_KEYWORDS.
36	 *   global_explicit  — Caller intentionally requested all domains (e.g. the
37	 *                      contradiction-watcher checking a claim with no taxonomy
38	 *                      tags yet, or a broad synthesis sweep).
39	 */
40	export type RetrievalPlanSearchScope =
41	  | 'domain_filtered'
42	  | 'global_fallback'
43	  | 'global_explicit';
44	
45	export type RetrievalPlan = {
46	  /** Up to 3 top-level domain IDs from knowledge_top_domains. Empty = search all. */
47	  topDomainHints: string[];
48	  /** Entity references the query is about; matched against canonical entity registry. */
49	  requiredEntities: { entityType: string; canonicalValue: string }[];
50	  /** Document classes to exclude from the candidate set (e.g. vendor_manual for system questions). */
51	  excludedDocumentClasses?: string[];
52	  /** Entity types whose presence in a claim should exclude it. */
53	  excludedEntityTypes?: string[];
54	  /** Top-level domain IDs to exclude from the search. */
55	  excludedTopDomains?: string[];
56	  /** Process stage hints for narrowing retrieval to a specific lifecycle stage. */
57	  processStageHints?: string[];
58	  /**
59	   * Time validity filter.
60	   *   'current' — only claims whose effective_until IS NULL (still in force).
61	   *   'all'     — no time filter.
62	   *   'since:YYYY-MM-DD' — claims effective after a given date.
63	   */
64	  timeFilter?: 'current' | 'all' | `since:${string}`;
65	  /** The actual semantic query string sent to the embedding + tsvector search. */
66	  vectorQuery: string;
67	  /** Maximum number of results to return. */
68	  topK: number;
69	  /**
70	   * How the retrieval scope was determined.
71	   * Used for observability — searchWithRetrievalPlan() warns on global_fallback.
72	   * Chat route stores this in oracle_context_packs.selected_domains as
73	   * '_global_fallback' so operators can query the fallback rate.
74	   */
75	  searchScope: RetrievalPlanSearchScope;
76	  /**
77	   * Optional pre-computed embedding for `vectorQuery`.
78	   * When present, searchWithRetrievalPlan() skips the embedText() call and uses
79	   * this vector directly, saving one OpenAI API round-trip.
80	   *
81	   * Must be exactly EMBEDDING_DIM (1536) floats.  The canonical use-case is
82	   * contradiction-watcher: it already computes/stores the claim embedding once
83	   * to persist it in `claims.embedding`, then threads the same vector here so
84	   * the subsequent ANN search doesn't embed the same summary a second time.
85	   */
86	  precomputedVector?: number[];
87	  /**
88	   * Department(s) of the employee making the request.
89	   * Used by searchWithRetrievalPlan() to apply a small RRF score bonus to
90	   * claims whose claim_metadata.department matches one of these values.
91	   * This is a soft signal — it nudges ranking but never filters results.
92	   * Sourced from employees.departments (the full array) at query time.
93	   */
94	  departmentHints?: string[];
95	};
96	
97	export const DEFAULT_TOP_K = 8;
98	
99	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
100	// Domain-hint heuristics.
101	// Keyword groups that map query text to top-level domain IDs.
102	// These domain IDs MUST match the `id` column in knowledge_top_domains
103	// (seeded in packages/db/migrations/sql/16_knowledge_top_domains_seed.sql).
104	//
105	// When a user query produces topDomainHints: [] (no keywords matched), the
106	// plan's searchScope is set to 'global_fallback' and a warning is logged.
107	// Add keywords here to reduce the fallback rate.
108	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
109	
110	const DOMAIN_KEYWORDS: Array<{ domainId: string; keywords: string[] }> = [
111	  {
112	    domainId: 'it_systems',

120	      // File / data operations
121	      'upload', 'sync', 'database', 'export', 'import', 'integration',
122	      'api', 'automat', 'process automation',
123	      // Asset types
124	      'image', 'photo', 'template', 'barcode', 'label', 'report',
125	      'document', 'spreadsheet',
126	      // Access / credentials
127	      'password', 'login', 'log in', 'sign in', 'account', 'credentials',
128	      'two-factor', '2fa', 'sso', 'access to', 'permission', 'user account',
129	      'drive',
130	      // Common IT question patterns
131	      'how does the system', 'how does it work', 'where do i',
132	    ],
133	  },
134	  {
135	    domainId: 'licensing_approvals',
136	    keywords: [
137	      // Licensors by name
138	      'disney', 'marvel', 'star wars', 'lucasfilm', 'warner bros', 'nbcuniversal',
139	      'nickelodeon', 'hasbro', 'mattel', 'sega', 'nintendo', 'universal',
140	      'dreamworks', 'sony pictures', 'paramount', 'nfl', 'nba', 'mlb', 'nhl',
141	      // License terms
142	      'licensor', 'license', 'licens', 'licensee', 'ip right',
143	      'trademark', 'copyright', 'brand', 'intellectual property',
144	      // Approval process
145	      'approval', 'approve', 'approved', 'submit', 'submission', 'resubmit',
146	      'round', 'revision', 'notes', 'art notes', 'feedback',
147	      'approval form', 'approval timeline', 'approval process', 'approval deadline',
148	      'digital sample', 'physical sample approval', 'pre-production approval',
149	      // Collateral
150	      'style guide', 'artwork approval', 'licensed', 'brand approval',
151	      'art approval', 'character art', 'licensed art', 'art submission',
152	      'print approval', 'color approval', 'color standard',
153	    ],
154	  },
155	  {
156	    domainId: 'customer_ops',
157	    keywords: [
158	      // Retailers by name
159	      'burlington', 'tjx', 'tj maxx', 'ross', 'hobby lobby', 'walmart',
160	      'target', 'amazon', 'five below', 'dollar tree', 'big lots',
161	      'marshalls', 'homegoods', 'tuesday morning', 'bealls', 'ollie',
162	      // Customer ops terms

164	      'retail compliance', 'compliance portal',

166	      'routing guide', 'chargeback', 'chargeback dispute', 'edi', 'compliance',
167	      'dc ', 'distribution center', 'store', 'floor-ready', 'floor ready',
168	      // Item setup and UPC

170	      'gtin', 'barcode setup', 'product setup',
171	      // Order management

173	      'planogram', 'retail price', 'sell-through', 'floor display',
174	    ],
175	  },
176	  {
177	    domainId: 'supply_chain',
178	    keywords: [
179	      // Geography / partners
180	      'factory', 'china', 'manufacturing', 'overseas', 'brazil', 'colombia',
181	      'vendor', 'supplier', 'vietnam', 'bangladesh', 'india', 'cambodia',

187	      'mold', 'tooling', 'raw material', 'component', 'bom', 'bill of material',
188	      'bill of materials',

191	      // Vendor management

193	      'factory performance', 'vendor performance', 'scorecard',
194	    ],
195	  },

197	    domainId: 'logistics_shipping',
198	    keywords: [

217	    ],
218	  },
219	  {
220	    domainId: 'product_development',
221	    keywords: [
222	      // Design / creative

226	      'material', 'fabric', 'color', 'colour', 'pantone', 'decoration',

292	    keywords: [

296	      // Customs agencies

300	      'certificate of origin', 'form a', 'coo certificate',

302	      'cpsc', 'astm', 'en71', 'safety test', 'product safety',
303	      'test report', 'test lab', 'testing lab', 'third party test',
304	      'lead test', 'flammability', 'prop 65', 'reach', 'rohs',

311	    keywords: [
312	      // Costs
313	      'cost', 'costing', 'fob cost', 'landed cost', 'unit cost',
314	      'duty cost', 'freight cost', 'all-in cost',
315	      // Pricing
316	      'price', 'pricing', 'wholesale', 'retail price', 'msrp',
317	      'markup', 'margin', 'gross margin', 'contribution margin',
318	      // Quotes and proformas
319	      'quote', 'quotation', 'proforma', 'proforma invoice', 'pi',
320	      'price list', 'price sheet',
321	      // Terms and payments
322	      'vendor terms', 'finance', 'payment', 'invoice', 'budget',
323	      'credit', 'payment terms', 'net 30', 'net 60', 'net 45',
324	      'wire transfer', 'bank transfer', 'wire', 'tt payment',
325	      'letter of credit', 'lc ', 'deposit', 'balance payment',
326	      'accounts payable', 'accounts receivable',
327	      // IP fees
328	      'royalty', 'royalties', 'licensing fee', 'advance', 'guarantee',
329	      'minimum guarantee', 'mg ',
330	    ],
331	  },
332	  {
333	    domainId: 'people_org',
334	    keywords: [
335	      // Org
336	      'employee', 'team', 'department', 'division', 'report to', 'org chart',
337	      'responsibility matrix', 'raci',
338	      // Questions about ownership
339	      'who handles', 'who is responsible', 'who owns', 'who should',
340	      'who manages', 'who do i contact', 'contact',
341	      // Handoffs and coverage
342	      'hand off', 'handoff', 'handover', 'hand over', 'assign', 'assigned to',
343	      'coverage', 'backup', 'vacation coverage', 'out of office', 'ooo',
344	      'covering for', 'delegate',
345	      // HR terms
346	      'escalat', 'manager', 'ownership', 'role', 'email',
347	      'point of contact', 'poc', 'new hire', 'onboard', 'onboarding',
348	    ],
349	  },
350	];
351	
352	// Entity type exclusion hints — inferred from query content.
353	const ENTITY_EXCLUSION_HINTS: Array<{ keywords: string[]; excludeEntityTypes: string[] }> = [
354	  {
355	    // Licensor questions → exclude generic vendors and factories from results.
356	    keywords: [
357	      'disney', 'marvel', 'star wars', 'lucasfilm', 'warner bros', 'nbcuniversal',
358	      'licensor', 'licens',
359	    ],
360	    excludeEntityTypes: ['vendor', 'factory', 'freight_provider'],
361	  },
362	  {
363	    // IT system questions → exclude licensor + vendor from results.
364	    keywords: ['erp', 'coldlion', 'system', 'software', 'tool', 'platform', 'portal'],
365	    excludeEntityTypes: ['licensor', 'vendor'],
366	  },
367	];
368	
369	// Document class exclusion hints — inferred from query content.
370	const DOCUMENT_CLASS_EXCLUSIONS: Array<{ keywords: string[]; excludeClasses: string[] }> = [
371	  {
372	    // IT system questions should not pull vendor manuals.
373	    keywords: ['erp', 'coldlion', 'system', 'software', 'tool', 'upload', 'sync', 'platform'],
374	    excludeClasses: ['vendor_manual'],
375	  },
376	  {
377	    // Licensing questions should not pull vendor manuals or generic routing guides.
378	    keywords: ['disney', 'marvel', 'licens', 'approval', 'style guide', 'art approval'],
379	    excludeClasses: ['vendor_manual', 'routing_guide'],
380	  },
381	];
382	
383	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
384	// Public builders
385	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
386	
387	/**
388	 * Build a RetrievalPlan from a natural-language query using lightweight
389	 * keyword heuristics. No model call — synchronous.
390	 *
391	 * Callers may pass explicit overrides (e.g. the Oracle tool caller already
392	 * knows the domains from context); those take priority over heuristic inference.
393	 *
394	 * Sets searchScope:
395	 *   'domain_filtered' — ≥1 domain matched.
396	 *   'global_fallback' — 0 domains matched; searchWithRetrievalPlan() will warn.
397	 *
398	 * @example
399	 * const plan = buildRetrievalPlanFromQuery(
400	 *   "When does an image get uploaded to the ERP system?",
401	 *   { topK: 10 }
402	 * );
403	 * // → topDomainHints: ['it_systems', 'production_lifecycle']
404	 * //   excludedDocumentClasses: ['vendor_manual']
405	 * //   excludedEntityTypes: ['licensor', 'vendor']
406	 * //   searchScope: 'domain_filtered'
407	 */
408	export function buildRetrievalPlanFromQuery(
409	  query: string,
410	  opts?: Partial<
411	    Pick<
412	      RetrievalPlan,
413	      | 'topDomainHints'
414	      | 'requiredEntities'
415	      | 'excludedDocumentClasses'
416	      | 'excludedEntityTypes'
417	      | 'topK'
418	      | 'timeFilter'
419	      | 'processStageHints'
420	      | 'precomputedVector'
421	      | 'departmentHints'
422	      | 'excludedTopDomains'
423	    >
424	  >,
425	): RetrievalPlan {
426	  const q = query.toLowerCase();
427	
428	  const inferredTopDomainHints: string[] =
429	    opts?.topDomainHints && opts.topDomainHints.length > 0
430	      ? opts.topDomainHints.slice(0, 3)
431	      : inferTopDomains(q);
432	
433	  const excludedEntityTypes: string[] =
434	    opts?.excludedEntityTypes && opts.excludedEntityTypes.length > 0
435	      ? opts.excludedEntityTypes
436	      : inferEntityExclusions(q);
437	
438	  const excludedDocumentClasses: string[] =
439	    opts?.excludedDocumentClasses && opts.excludedDocumentClasses.length > 0
440	      ? opts.excludedDocumentClasses
441	      : inferDocumentClassExclusions(q);
442	
443	  const excludedTopDomains: string[] =
444	    opts?.excludedTopDomains && opts.excludedTopDomains.length > 0
445	      ? opts.excludedTopDomains
446	      : inferTopDomainExclusions(q, inferredTopDomainHints);
447	
448	  const topDomainHints =
449	    excludedTopDomains.length > 0
450	      ? inferredTopDomainHints.filter((d) => !excludedTopDomains.includes(d)).slice(0, 3)
451	      : inferredTopDomainHints;
452	
453	  const searchScope: RetrievalPlanSearchScope =
454	    topDomainHints.length > 0 ? 'domain_filtered' : 'global_fallback';
455	
456	  return {
457	    topDomainHints,
458	    requiredEntities: opts?.requiredEntities ?? [],
459	    excludedDocumentClasses: excludedDocumentClasses.length > 0 ? excludedDocumentClasses : undefined,
460	    excludedEntityTypes: excludedEntityTypes.length > 0 ? excludedEntityTypes : undefined,
461	    excludedTopDomains: excludedTopDomains.length > 0 ? excludedTopDomains : undefined,
462	    processStageHints: opts?.processStageHints,
463	    timeFilter: opts?.timeFilter ?? 'current',
464	    vectorQuery: query,
465	    topK: opts?.topK ?? DEFAULT_TOP_K,
466	    searchScope,
467	    precomputedVector: opts?.precomputedVector,
468	    departmentHints: opts?.departmentHints,
469	  };
470	}
471	
472	/**
473	 * Build a RetrievalPlan with explicitly-provided domain hints and NO heuristic
474	 * entity/document-class exclusion inference.
475	 *
476	 * Use this when the caller already knows the exact domains (e.g. contradiction-
477	 * watcher passing the target claim's claim_top_domains). Avoids accidental
478	 * exclusions when the intent is to search broadly within known domains.
479	 *
480	 * Sets searchScope: 'domain_filtered' if domainHints is non-empty,
481	 *                   'global_fallback' if empty (caller should use buildGlobalRetrievalPlan instead).
482	 */
483	export function buildDomainScopedPlan(
484	  query: string,
485	  topDomainHints: string[],
486	  opts?: Partial<
487	    Pick<
488	      RetrievalPlan,
489	      | 'topK'
490	      | 'timeFilter'
491	      | 'requiredEntities'
492	      | 'excludedDocumentClasses'
493	      | 'excludedEntityTypes'
494	      | 'precomputedVector'
495	    >
496	  >,
497	): RetrievalPlan {
498	  return {
499	    topDomainHints: topDomainHints.slice(0, 3),
500	    requiredEntities: opts?.requiredEntities ?? [],
501	    excludedDocumentClasses: opts?.excludedDocumentClasses,
502	    excludedEntityTypes: opts?.excludedEntityTypes,
503	    excludedTopDomains: undefined,
504	    processStageHints: undefined,
505	    timeFilter: opts?.timeFilter ?? 'current',
506	    vectorQuery: query,
507	    topK: opts?.topK ?? DEFAULT_TOP_K,
508	    searchScope: topDomainHints.length > 0 ? 'domain_filtered' : 'global_fallback',
509	    precomputedVector: opts?.precomputedVector,
510	  };
511	}
512	
513	/**
514	 * Build a RetrievalPlan that intentionally searches the full claim corpus.
515	 *
516	 * Use when the caller explicitly wants all domains — e.g. a broad synthesis
517	 * sweep, a re-indexing job, or a diagnostic search. Marks searchScope as
518	 * 'global_explicit' so monitoring can distinguish intentional global searches
519	 * from heuristic failures (global_fallback).
520	 *
521	 * Do NOT use this for ordinary chat-route retrieval. Use buildRetrievalPlanFromQuery
522	 * so the query goes through domain inference and gets a domain_filtered scope.
523	 */
524	export function buildGlobalRetrievalPlan(
525	  query: string,
526	  opts?: Partial<
527	    Pick<
528	      RetrievalPlan,
529	      | 'topK'
530	      | 'timeFilter'
531	      | 'excludedDocumentClasses'
532	      | 'requiredEntities'
533	      | 'precomputedVector'
534	    >
535	  >,
536	): RetrievalPlan {
537	  return {
538	    topDomainHints: [],
539	    requiredEntities: opts?.requiredEntities ?? [],
540	    excludedDocumentClasses: opts?.excludedDocumentClasses,
541	    excludedEntityTypes: undefined,
542	    excludedTopDomains: undefined,
543	    processStageHints: undefined,
544	    timeFilter: opts?.timeFilter ?? 'current',
545	    vectorQuery: query,
546	    topK: opts?.topK ?? DEFAULT_TOP_K,
547	    searchScope: 'global_explicit',
548	    precomputedVector: opts?.precomputedVector,
549	  };
550	}
551	
552	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
553	// Private heuristic helpers
554	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
555	
556	function inferTopDomains(query: string): string[] {
557	  const scores = new Map<string, number>();
558	  for (const { domainId, keywords } of DOMAIN_KEYWORDS) {
559	    const score = keywords.filter((k) => query.includes(k)).length;
560	    if (score > 0) scores.set(domainId, score);
561	  }
562	  return Array.from(scores.entries())
563	    .sort((a, b) => b[1] - a[1])
564	    .slice(0, 3)
565	    .map(([id]) => id);
566	}
567	
568	function inferEntityExclusions(query: string): string[] {
569	  const excluded = new Set<string>();
570	  for (const { keywords, excludeEntityTypes } of ENTITY_EXCLUSION_HINTS) {
571	    if (keywords.some((k) => query.includes(k))) {
572	      for (const t of excludeEntityTypes) excluded.add(t);
573	    }
574	  }
575	  return Array.from(excluded);
576	}
577	
578	function inferDocumentClassExclusions(query: string): string[] {
579	  const excluded = new Set<string>();
580	  for (const { keywords, excludeClasses } of DOCUMENT_CLASS_EXCLUSIONS) {
581	    if (keywords.some((k) => query.includes(k))) {
582	      for (const c of excludeClasses) excluded.add(c);
583	    }
584	  }
585	  return Array.from(excluded);
586	}
587	
588	function inferTopDomainExclusions(query: string, topDomainHints: string[]): string[] {
589	  if (!topDomainHints.includes('design_file_operations')) return [];
590	
591	  const looksLikeFileOps =
592	    [
593	      'file naming', 'filename', 'file name', 'invalid character', 'save files',
594	      'saving files', 'bloated', 'file size', 'folder structure', 'shared folder',
595	      'server folder', 'design server', 'linked asset', 'embedded asset',
596	      'missing link', 'package links', 'artwork file', 'design files',
597	      'designer files', 'creative files',
598	    ].some((k) => query.includes(k));
599	
600	  if (!looksLikeFileOps) return [];
601	
602	  // File-operations questions are about keeping creative files usable, small,
603	  // compatible, and findable. They should not silently drift into product
604	  // approval/status workflow unless the user asks that workflow question.
605	  return ['product_development', 'production_lifecycle', 'it_systems'];
606	}
607

content.content: No files found

276	## Teams live participation (Recall.ai)
277	
278	Adds live Teams meeting awareness without changing the Vercel/Supabase/Trigger.dev infrastructure. Recall.ai owns the meeting bot and audio/STT transport; The Oracle receives finalized transcript utterances and decides whether to ask a short meeting-chat question.
279	
280	Status: **LIVE + validated end-to-end (2026-06-08/09).** The tested path is: admin start route -> Recall Teams bot join -> ElevenLabs/AssemblyAI live STT -> signed `/api/teams/live/recall` webhook -> Trigger.dev `teams-live-recall-utterance` -> `messages` persistence -> Recall `send_chat_message` -> visible Teams chat post. The latest tested worker deployment is `20260609.6` after removing the temporary test-only bot-create task.
281	
282	After the live test, posting was deliberately clamped off in `settings` (`max_oracle_interjections_per_hour=0`, `teams_live_recall_min_confidence_to_post=101`, force flags false). The mechanical live path works; the next product gap is context quality. Today the worker reasons over the current utterance plus a recent meeting window, not approved claims, Brain sections, or older relevant messages. Add retrieval through the existing `searchWithRetrievalPlan()` path before expecting the live Oracle to ask deeply informed operational questions.
283	
284	```
285	Admin POST /api/teams/live/start { meetingUrl, provider }
286	  provider: elevenlabs_streaming first, assembly_ai_v3_streaming fallback
287	  ↓
288	apps/web/lib/recall.ts creates Recall bot with realtime_endpoints=[/api/teams/live/recall]
289	  ↓
290	Recall bot joins Teams meeting, transcribes speech live
291	  ↓ finalized transcript.data event (not partials)
292	apps/web/app/api/teams/live/recall/route.ts
293	  • verify Recall whsec_ signature
294	  • triggerTask('teams-live-recall-utterance', { event })
295	  ↓
296	apps/workers/src/trigger/teams-live-recall-utterance.ts
297	  • normalize utterance words -> text
298	  • create/find per-bot Oracle channel
299	  • resolve speaker by email/name when available
300	  • insert messages row: role='user', source='teams_live_recall'
301	  • keyword gate skips low-signal utterances
302	  • interview-route LLM decides whether to ask one short question
303	  • rate/cooldown gates via oracle_interventions
304	  • Recall send_chat_message posts the question to Teams chat
305	```
306	
307	This path uses finalized utterances only. STT partials are provisional hypotheses while someone is still speaking; acting on them would make Oracle interrupt on half-heard sentences. Post-call Graph transcripts remain the canonical backstop for complete evidence if live bot delivery drops events.
308	
309	### Teams-native command wrapper
310	
311	The Recall listener itself cannot be invited from the Teams people picker as an internal employee. To let employees initiate it from Teams, The Oracle also exposes a Bot Framework endpoint:
312	
313	```
314	Teams user adds The Oracle app to a meeting/chat
315	  ↓
316	@The Oracle join <Teams meeting link>
317	  ↓
318	Azure Bot Service -> /api/teams/bot/messages
319	  • verifies Bot Framework auth via MICROSOFT_BOT_APP_ID/PASSWORD
320	  • strips the @mention and parses join/listen/start commands
321	  • uses Teams meeting context for joinUrl when available
322	  • otherwise asks the user to include the meeting link
323	  • creates the Recall live bot
324	```
325	
326	So the user-facing flow originates inside Teams, while the live audio/STT work is still performed by Recall.
327	
328	Current production wiring (2026-06-09): Azure Bot resource `theoracle-popcre-teams-bot` routes the Microsoft Teams channel to `https://oracle.designflow.app/api/teams/bot/messages`; the Teams organization app `The Oracle` is uploaded with app id `17ccd7a1-b90b-428c-9966-33e7fb832923`. This does not replace Recall.ai for live audio/STT; it only provides the native Teams command surface.
329	
330	## Identity model
331	
332	One human → one `employees` row → many `employee_identities` rows.
333	
334	- `employees.email` is the **primary contact** for the human (used for display, admin contact, and first-login bootstrap).
335	- `employee_identities` is the authoritative source for `(auth_provider, auth_user_id)`. Supabase Auth identifies the user; the linker maps that Supabase user to the employee row through this table.
336	- The linker resolves a session by `(auth_provider, auth_user_id)` first. On miss it bootstraps by matching the verified provider email against `employees.email` OR any existing `employee_identities.email` row, then creates a new identity. See `packages/auth/src/link.ts`.
337	- The RLS helper `current_employee_id()` joins `employees` with `employee_identities` on `auth.uid()` — RLS does not read `employees.auth_user_id` directly.
338	- Deprecated columns `employees.auth_user_id`, `employees.auth_provider`, `employees.auth_provider_subject` remain on the schema as NULL placeholders during the multi-identity transition. They will be dropped in a follow-up migration. See `DECISIONS.md` D2.multi-identity.
339	- `employees.departments text[]` (multi-value, authoritative) replaces `employees.department varchar` (single-value, nullable, deprecated). All new code reads `departments`; legacy code falls back to `department`. The retrieval layer uses `departments` as `departmentHints` — a soft RRF signal, never a hard filter.
340	
341	## Data flow — the load-bearing paths
342	
343	### 1. Employee sends a message
344	
345	1. Browser inserts into `messages` via the Supabase client (RLS enforced — must be a participant of the channel).
346	2. Supabase Realtime fan-outs `postgres_changes` to other participants.
347	3. If the message starts with `@oracle` (or `oracle,`), the client calls `POST /api/chat` (see flow 2).
348	4. The message row sits with `extraction_status='pending'`. The claim extraction worker (Phase 4) picks it up later.
349	
350	### 2. `@oracle` mention → chat response
351	
352	1. `POST /api/chat` receives `{ channelId }`. The employee is resolved server-side from the Supabase session cookie — the client does not pass an employee ID.
353	2. The route resolves the requester's `employees` row through `employee_identities` (matches `auth.uid()` from the Supabase session). Verifies the requester is a participant of `channelId`.
354	3. Classifies the query via `buildRetrievalPlanFromQuery` (heuristic keyword → `topDomainHints`, `requiredEntities`, `excludedDocumentClasses`, `searchScope`). Passes the employee's `departments` array as `departmentHints` — a soft signal added to the RRF score (+0.002 per claim whose `claim_metadata.department` matches). Runs hybrid pgvector + tsvector RRF via `searchWithRetrievalPlan` with metadata pre-filter. Also fetches recent N messages, employee profile, and top open gaps for this employee/department.
355	4. Calls `OracleAIClient.runText` with the spec Part 10 system prompt + the retrieval bundle. Route is `settings.default_interview_route` (default `anthropic_claude_haiku_4_5_interview_primary`), dispatched through the direct `AnthropicAdapter` (`@anthropic-ai/sdk`). Tools, multi-turn `messages`, `stopWhen`, and `temperature` are passed through the `providerOptions` escape hatch. Image/file parts are stripped for text-only models before the call.
356	5. Tools exposed: `search_company_knowledge`, `check_open_gaps` — both Zod-validated, both backed by `packages/ai/src/retrieval.ts`.
357	6. On completion: inserts the assistant message into `messages` and writes a `model_runs` row with cost/latency/tokens.
358	
359	### 3. Document upload
360	
361	1. Browser uploads file to Supabase Storage bucket `company_documents`.
362	2. Creates a `documents` row (`status='pending_processing'`).
363	3. Creates a `message_attachments` row linking the document to the message that referenced it.
364	4. After the upload completes, the client triggers `POST /api/chat` — same Oracle reply flow as flow 2. In DMs this always fires; in group chats it fires only when the upload caption starts with `@oracle`.
365	5. The document ingestion worker (Phase 4) picks up `status='pending_processing'`, chunks the file into `document_chunks`, embeds them, then runs claim extraction over the chunks.

file.content:
## Teams live participation (Recall.ai)

Adds live Teams meeting awareness without changing the Vercel/Supabase/Trigger.dev infrastructure. Recall.ai owns the meeting bot and audio/STT transport; The Oracle receives finalized transcript utterances and decides whether to ask a short meeting-chat question.

Status: **LIVE + validated end-to-end (2026-06-08/09).** The tested path is: admin start route -> Recall Teams bot join -> ElevenLabs/AssemblyAI live STT -> signed `/api/teams/live/recall` webhook -> Trigger.dev `teams-live-recall-utterance` -> `messages` persistence -> Recall `send_chat_message` -> visible Teams chat post. The latest tested worker deployment is `20260609.6` after removing the temporary test-only bot-create task.

After the live test, posting was deliberately clamped off in `settings` (`max_oracle_interjections_per_hour=0`, `teams_live_recall_min_confidence_to_post=101`, force flags false). The mechanical live path works; the next product gap is context quality. Today the worker reasons over the current utterance plus a recent meeting window, not approved claims, Brain sections, or older relevant messages. Add retrieval through the existing `searchWithRetrievalPlan()` path before expecting the live Oracle to ask deeply informed operational questions.

```
Admin POST /api/teams/live/start { meetingUrl, provider }
  provider: elevenlabs_streaming first, assembly_ai_v3_streaming fallback
  ↓
apps/web/lib/recall.ts creates Recall bot with realtime_endpoints=[/api/teams/live/recall]
  ↓
Recall bot joins Teams meeting, transcribes speech live
  ↓ finalized transcript.data event (not partials)
apps/web/app/api/teams/live/recall/route.ts
  • verify Recall whsec_ signature
  • triggerTask('teams-live-recall-utterance', { event })
  ↓
apps/workers/src/trigger/teams-live-recall-utterance.ts
  • normalize utterance words -> text
  • create/find per-bot Oracle channel
  • resolve speaker by email/name when available
  • insert messages row: role='user', source='teams_live_recall'
  • keyword gate skips low-signal utterances
  • interview-route LLM decides whether to ask one short question
  • rate/cooldown gates via oracle_interventions
  • Recall send_chat_message posts the question to Teams chat
```

This path uses finalized utterances only. STT partials are provisional hypotheses while someone is still speaking; acting on them would make Oracle interrupt on half-heard sentences. Post-call Graph transcripts remain the canonical backstop for complete evidence if live bot delivery drops events.

### Teams-native command wrapper

The Recall listener itself cannot be invited from the Teams people picker as an internal employee. To let employees initiate it from Teams, The Oracle also exposes a Bot Framework endpoint:

```
Teams user adds The Oracle app to a meeting/chat
  ↓
@The Oracle join <Teams meeting link>
  ↓
Azure Bot Service -> /api/teams/bot/messages
  • verifies Bot Framework auth via MICROSOFT_BOT_APP_ID/PASSWORD
  • strips the @mention and parses join/listen/start commands
  • uses Teams meeting context for joinUrl when available
  • otherwise asks the user to include the meeting link
  • creates the Recall live bot
```

So the user-facing flow originates inside Teams, while the live audio/STT work is still performed by Recall.

Current production wiring (2026-06-09): Azure Bot resource `theoracle-popcre-teams-bot` routes the Microsoft Teams channel to `https://oracle.designflow.app/api/teams/bot/messages`; the Teams organization app `The Oracle` is uploaded with app id `17ccd7a1-b90b-428c-9966-33e7fb832923`. This does not replace Recall.ai for live audio/STT; it only provides the native Teams command surface.

## Identity model

One human → one `employees` row → many `employee_identities` rows.

- `employees.email` is the **primary contact** for the human (used for display, admin contact, and first-login bootstrap).
- `employee_identities` is the authoritative source for `(auth_provider, auth_user_id)`. Supabase Auth identifies the user; the linker maps that Supabase user to the employee row through this table.
- The linker resolves a session by `(auth_provider, auth_user_id)` first. On miss it bootstraps by matching the verified provider email against `employees.email` OR any existing `employee_identities.email` row, then creates a new identity. See `packages/auth/src/link.ts`.
- The RLS helper `current_employee_id()` joins `employees` with `employee_identities` on `auth.uid()` — RLS does not read `employees.auth_user_id` directly.
- Deprecated columns `employees.auth_user_id`, `employees.auth_provider`, `employees.auth_provider_subject` remain on the schema as NULL placeholders during the multi-identity transition. They will be dropped in a follow-up migration. See `DECISIONS.md` D2.multi-identity.
- `employees.departments text[]` (multi-value, authoritative) replaces `employees.department varchar` (single-value, nullable, deprecated). All new code reads `departments`; legacy code falls back to `department`. The retrieval layer uses `departments` as `departmentHints` — a soft RRF signal, never a hard filter.

## Data flow — the load-bearing paths

### 1. Employee sends a message

1. Browser inserts into `messages` via the Supabase client (RLS enforced — must be a participant of the channel).
2. Supabase Realtime fan-outs `postgres_changes` to other participants.
3. If the message starts with `@oracle` (or `oracle,`), the client calls `POST /api/chat` (see flow 2).
4. The message row sits with `extraction_status='pending'`. The claim extraction worker (Phase 4) picks it up later.

### 2. `@oracle` mention → chat response

1. `POST /api/chat` receives `{ channelId }`. The employee is resolved server-side from the Supabase session cookie — the client does not pass an employee ID.
2. The route resolves the requester's `employees` row through `employee_identities` (matches `auth.uid()` from the Supabase session). Verifies the requester is a participant of `channelId`.
3. Classifies the query via `buildRetrievalPlanFromQuery` (heuristic keyword → `topDomainHints`, `requiredEntities`, `excludedDocumentClasses`, `searchScope`). Passes the employee's `departments` array as `departmentHints` — a soft signal added to the RRF score (+0.002 per claim whose `claim_metadata.department` matches). Runs hybrid pgvector + tsvector RRF via `searchWithRetrievalPlan` with metadata pre-filter. Also fetches recent N messages, employee profile, and top open gaps for this employee/department.
4. Calls `OracleAIClient.runText` with the spec Part 10 system prompt + the retrieval bundle. Route is `settings.default_interview_route` (default `anthropic_claude_haiku_4_5_interview_primary`), dispatched through the direct `AnthropicAdapter` (`@anthropic-ai/sdk`). Tools, multi-turn `messages`, `stopWhen`, and `temperature` are passed through the `providerOptions` escape hatch. Image/file parts are stripped for text-only models before the call.
5. Tools exposed: `search_company_knowledge`, `check_open_gaps` — both Zod-validated, both backed by `packages/ai/src/retrieval.ts`.
6. On completion: inserts the assistant message into `messages` and writes a `model_runs` row with cost/latency/tokens.

### 3. Document upload

1. Browser uploads file to Supabase Storage bucket `company_documents`.
2. Creates a `documents` row (`status='pending_processing'`).
3. Creates a `message_attachments` row linking the document to the message that referenced it.
4. After the upload completes, the client triggers `POST /api/chat` — same Oracle reply flow as flow 2. In DMs this always fires; in group chats it fires only when the upload caption starts with `@oracle`.
5. The document ingestion worker (Phase 4) picks up `status='pending_processing'`, chunks the file into `document_chunks`, embeds them, then runs claim extraction over the chunks.

input.command:
$sig = @'
[DllImport("advapi32.dll", SetLastError=true, CharSet=CharSet.Unicode)]
public static extern bool CredRead(string target, int type, int flags, out IntPtr cred);
[DllImport("advapi32.dll")]
public static extern void CredFree(IntPtr cred);
'@
$t = Add-Type -MemberDefinition $sig -Name CredMan -Namespace Win32 -PassThru
$ptr = [IntPtr]::Zero
if ([Win32.CredMan]::CredRead('Supabase CLI:supabase', 1, 0, [ref]$ptr)) {
  $cred = [System.Runtime.InteropServices.Marshal]::PtrToStructure($ptr, [type]'System.IntPtr')
  # CREDENTIAL struct: read blob size at offset and blob pointer
  $blobSize = [System.Runtime.InteropServices.Marshal]::ReadInt32($ptr, 36 + 2*[IntPtr]::Size)
  $blobPtr = [System.Runtime.InteropServices.Marshal]::ReadIntPtr($ptr, 40 + 2*[IntPtr]::Size)
  $bytes = New-Object byte[] $blobSize
  [System.Runtime.InteropServices.Marshal]::Copy($blobPtr, $bytes, 0, $blobSize)
  $token = [System.Text.Encoding]::UTF8.GetString($bytes)
  $env:SBP_TOKEN = $token
  "token length: $($token.Length); prefix: $($token.Substring(0,4))"
  [Win32.CredMan]::CredFree($ptr)
} else {
  "CredRead failed: $([System.Runtime.InteropServices.Marshal]::GetLastWin32Error())"
}
